cohorts.label: Sponsor A and Sponsor E joint bidder group
Sponsor E and Sponsor G joint bidder group
58 potentially interested third parties contacted during go shop process
Six parties that expressed interest during go shop

observations.summary: Board and senior management conducted periodic strategic review with Goldman Sachs participation in December 2012
observations.other_detail: strategic_review

observations.summary: Sponsor A made unsolicited phone call to Saks CEO expressing interest in potential acquisition of Saks
observations.other_detail: unsolicited phone call

observations.summary: Saks CEO met with senior representatives of Sponsor A to discuss possibility of a potential transaction; no specific proposals or terms discussed
observations.other_detail: preliminary meeting, no proposals made

observations.summary: Board meeting: discussed contacts with Sponsor A, potential acquisition of Saks, and potential strategic acquisition of Company B; delegated ongoing updates to Finance committee
observations.other_detail: board discussed potential strategic transactions including acquisition of Company B

observations.summary: Hudsons Bay CEO Richard Baker met with Saks CEO and discussed a potential acquisition of Saks by Hudsons Bay; no specific proposals or terms discussed
observations.other_detail: meeting at request of Mr. Baker; no specific proposals made

observations.summary: Board meeting: reviewed expressions of interest from Hudsons Bay and Sponsor A; directed management to continue exploring potential transactions
observations.other_detail: board directed continued exploration

observations.summary: Finance and Executive committees held special joint meeting with Goldman Sachs and Wachtell Lipton; reviewed discussions with Hudsons Bay and Sponsor A; directed continued evaluation
observations.other_detail: special joint committee meeting

observations.summary: Following joint committee meeting, Goldman Sachs met separately with Sponsor C and Sponsor D about providing equity financing for acquisition of Company B
observations.other_detail: equity financing discussions for Company B acquisition

observations.summary: During week of April 15, each of Hudsons Bay and Sponsor A indicated considering making an offer to acquire Saks for at least $15 per share in cash; Sponsor E participated in meeting with Sponsor A
observations.other_detail: preliminary indications of interest communicated verbally via Goldman Sachs meetings

observations.summary: Saks and Sponsor C entered into a confidentiality agreement
Saks and Sponsor D entered into a confidentiality agreement
Board meeting: Goldman Sachs discussed alternatives available to Saks including acquisition by a third party, acquisition of Company B, and remaining independent
observations.other_detail: three alternatives discussed: sale, acquire Company B, remain independent

observations.summary: Saks entered into a confidentiality agreement with each of Sponsor A and Sponsor E
observations.other_detail: considering participating in a potential joint acquisition of Saks

observations.summary: Saks and Hudsons Bay entered into a confidentiality agreement
Following NDA executions, Saks made online dataroom available to Sponsor A, Sponsor E and Hudsons Bay; all three engaged in due diligence over next several weeks
observations.other_detail: dataroom access and due diligence commenced

observations.summary: Finance committee meeting: discussed Hudsons Bay request to contact potential equity financing sources; committee agreed to grant request subject to confidentiality agreement terms
observations.other_detail: granted Hudsons Bay permission to contact equity financing sources

observations.summary: Goldman Sachs orally communicated Saks preliminary non-binding proposal to acquire Company B in cash transaction with flexibility for stock consideration; confirmed in letter on May 20; Company B principal shareholders said proposal was insufficient
observations.other_detail: Target reverse-acquisition proposal: Goldman Sachs orally communicated Saks preliminary non-binding proposal to acqui

observations.summary: Media reports appeared stating Saks had engaged Goldman Sachs to explore a potential sale of Saks
observations.other_detail: late May 2013 media reports

observations.summary: Hudsons Bay indicated preliminarily prepared to submit proposal at $15 to $15.25 per share; Sponsor A and Sponsor E indicated $15 to $16 per share
observations.other_detail: indicative price range communicated via Goldman Sachs

observations.summary: Sponsor A and Sponsor E indicated preliminarily prepared to submit proposal at $15 to $16 per share of common stock
observations.other_detail: indicative price range communicated via Goldman Sachs

observations.summary: Board authorized implementation of a process to determine whether a transaction with a potential acquiror could be reached on terms offering attractive value to shareholders, and authorized submission of a revised proposal to acquire Company B
observations.other_detail: formal authorization of sale process for Saks and revised Company B proposal

observations.summary: Saks submitted a revised and non-binding proposal with a higher price to acquire Company B in a cash transaction with flexibility for stock consideration
observations.other_detail: Target reverse-acquisition proposal: Saks submitted a revised and non-binding proposal with a higher price to acquire

observations.summary: Company F indicated interest in participating with Sponsor A and Sponsor E in a potential acquisition of Saks; engaged in due diligence but did not participate in final offer
observations.other_detail: did not participate in the offer ultimately submitted by Sponsor A and Sponsor G

observations.summary: Goldman Sachs distributed draft merger agreement and process details to Hudsons Bay, Sponsor A and Sponsor E with request for submission of offers for all-cash acquisition of Saks no later than July 11, 2013
observations.other_detail: included draft merger agreement and request for comments

observations.summary: Sponsor E informed Saks that Sponsor A was no longer intending to be primary participant; Sponsor E entered discussions with Sponsor G for potential joint acquisition of Saks
observations.other_detail: early July 2013; Sponsor A stepping back from primary role, Sponsor E partnering with Sponsor G

observations.summary: Saks entered into a confidentiality agreement with Sponsor G
Hudsons Bay submitted proposal at $15.25 per share of common stock, including a revised draft merger agreement and committed debt and equity financing documentation
observations.other_detail: included committed debt and equity financing documentation

observations.summary: Sponsor E and Sponsor G submitted joint proposal with indicative price range of $14.50-$15.50 per share; noted need for several more weeks of due diligence; did not include revised draft merger agreement or financing documents
observations.other_detail: indicative price range; no financing documentation; needed several more weeks for diligence

observations.summary: Saks informed that Sponsor G was no longer participating in the process; Sponsor E would again be joined by Sponsor A as primary participant
observations.other_detail: Sponsor A returned as primary participant replacing Sponsor G

observations.summary: Company B principal shareholders indicated Saks proposed price was still insufficient and stated minimum required price; suggested management meeting
observations.other_detail: Saks determined not to meet with Company B management due to substantial differences on price

observations.summary: Goldman Sachs indicated to Sponsor A and Sponsor E that a purchase price in the lower half of the previously submitted range would not be acceptable in light of Hudsons Bay $15.25 bid
observations.other_detail: board direction; minimum price guidance relative to Hudsons Bay bid

observations.summary: Board meeting: reviewed Hudsons Bay proposal and Sponsor E/Sponsor A joint proposal; authorized Goldman Sachs to inform both sides their price proposals were insufficient; authorized Wachtell Lipton to provide revised merger agreement draft to Hudsons Bay
observations.other_detail: board found both initial price proposals insufficient

observations.summary: Wachtell Lipton provided a revised draft of the merger agreement to representatives of Hudsons Bay
observations.other_detail: revised draft; negotiations ongoing

observations.summary: Saks entered into a confidentiality agreement with Company B for Saks to engage in due diligence
observations.other_detail: Saks to perform due diligence on Company B

observations.summary: Company H sent letter purporting to propose to acquire Saks for an aggregate price of $2.6 billion in cash with no details or further information
observations.other_detail: unsolicited; Company H unknown to Saks and advisors; Goldman Sachs unable to make contact with Company H; no subsequent communications

observations.summary: Sponsor A and Sponsor E indicated willingness to continue pursuing transaction above $15 per share but believed it unlikely they would achieve value above the top end of their $14.50-$15.50 range
observations.other_detail: cannot-improve signal; unlikely to exceed $15.50 per share

observations.summary: Board meeting: concluded unable to agree on terms with Company B; authorized communication to Hudsons Bay that price must be at least $16 per share and significant merger agreement issues must be resolved
observations.other_detail: board set $16 per share floor for Hudsons Bay negotiations

observations.summary: Goldman Sachs informed Hudsons Bay financial advisor that Hudsons Bay would need to increase per share price to at least $16 and communicated Saks position on significant merger agreement and financing issues
observations.other_detail: minimum price floor of $16 per share communicated

observations.summary: Sponsor A and Sponsor E met with Saks management; no indication they were prepared to increase price beyond $14.50-$15.50 range or conclude diligence more quickly
observations.other_detail: evening meeting; cannot-improve signal on both price and timeline

observations.summary: Hudsons Bay advised Goldman Sachs it was prepared to offer $16 per share of common stock, subject to negotiating a definitive binding agreement, and was agreeable to Saks position on substantially all significant merger agreement and financing issues
observations.other_detail: subject to negotiating definitive binding agreement

observations.summary: Board meeting: Goldman Sachs informed board that Company B negotiations had ceased; board authorized senior management and advisors to proceed with Hudsons Bay negotiations
observations.other_detail: board authorized exclusive Hudsons Bay negotiations

observations.summary: Board meeting attended by Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley and Wachtell Lipton; reviewed terms of Hudsons Bay proposal and discussed status of negotiations
observations.other_detail: Morgan Stanley first mentioned as long-time advisor to Saks

observations.summary: Board meeting: reviewed Hudsons Bay proposal terms versus remaining standalone; Goldman Sachs delivered fairness opinion that $16 per share was fair to holders of common stock
observations.other_detail: Goldman Sachs fairness opinion delivered

observations.summary: Following board approval, Saks, Hudsons Bay and Merger Sub finalized and executed the merger agreement and other transaction documents
Saks and Hudsons Bay issued joint press release announcing entry into the transaction
observations.other_detail: public announcement

observations.summary: Go shop period commenced under merger agreement; Goldman Sachs contacted 58 potentially interested third parties on behalf of Saks
observations.other_detail: go shop process; contacted private equity firms, retail companies, and other potential acquirors

observations.summary: Company I executed a confidentiality agreement with Saks and conducted due diligence investigation during go shop; only one of six interested parties to do so
observations.other_detail: during go shop period; exact date not stated

observations.summary: Go shop period ended; no party designated as excluded party; no acquisition proposal submitted by any go shop contact including Company I
observations.other_detail: go shop concluded with no alternative proposals

observations.summary: As of proxy statement date, Saks has not received any communication regarding a potential alternative transaction that the board determined constitutes a superior proposal under the merger agreement
observations.other_detail: no superior proposal received as of proxy date

observations.summary: Saks informed Company B principal shareholders it was not willing to make an offer at or above minimum price; discussions of Company B acquisition ceased
observations.other_detail: Saks terminated Company B acquisition discussions

exclusions.explanation: Company B acquisition discussions by Saks: Company B is a potential acquisition target of Saks, not a bidder for Saks. Interactions with Company B are recorded as context observations but Company B is not modeled as a bidder.

Post-closing employment terms: Filing explicitly states negotiations with Hudsons Bay, Sponsor A and Sponsor E did not include any discussions regarding post-closing employment terms for executives or senior management.

Sponsor C and Sponsor D equity financing terms for Company B: Sponsor C and Sponsor D were potential equity financing providers for Saks acquisition of Company B, not bidders for Saks. Their NDAs are recorded but they are not modeled as Saks bidders.

Company I post-go-shop correspondence details: Company I raised issues about go shop duration and information access; Saks replied pointing out delays by Company I. No acquisition proposal was submitted. Correspondence details excluded as procedural dispute, not a bid event.
